title: HTML to Markdown Converter
shortDescription: Convert HTML to Markdown in your browser. Headings, lists, code, links, images, blockquotes, bold/italic/strikethrough.
description: Paste HTML and get clean Markdown. Useful for migrating CMS content, scraping articles, or converting WYSIWYG output to plain markdown for static sites.
metaDescription: Free HTML to Markdown converter. Headings, lists, code blocks, links, blockquotes, bold/italic. Output is plain Markdown ready to paste anywhere.

sections.heading: What gets converted
paragraphs:
Headings (h1-h6) become # to ######. / become **bold**, / become *italic*. becomes `inline code`, 
 becomes a fenced ``` block. 
    /
       become - or 1. lists.  becomes [text](url),  becomes ![alt](url). 
       becomes >, 
       becomes ---. 
       becomes a newline.
Anything not recognized (custom HTML like <details>, <table>, <video>, etc.) is stripped of tags but the text content is preserved. For complex HTML with tables or media, expect some loss.

sections.heading: Common use cases
paragraphs: CMS migration: copy WYSIWYG HTML out of WordPress, Wix, etc., and convert to clean Markdown for use in static-site generators (Hugo, Jekyll, Next.js, Astro).
Article archiving: save web articles as Markdown for personal reading. Combine with a paywall-friendly extractor (Reader mode HTML) for clean text-only output.
Email-to-Markdown: paste HTML email content and get a Markdown version for cross-posting to your blog.

sections.heading: Limitations
paragraphs: Tables: not converted (Markdown tables are GFM, not CommonMark). Tables are stripped to plain text. For complex tables, paste as HTML instead.
Inline styles: any color, font, or layout via <style> or style='' attributes is lost. Markdown is intentionally minimal.
Nested lists: simple single-level lists work; deeply nested lists may merge or lose structure. For complex outlines, hand-edit after conversion.

howTo.name: Paste HTML
howTo.text: From any source — WordPress export, copied web article, etc.

howTo.name: Copy and paste elsewhere
howTo.text: Output is plain Markdown ready for any static-site or markdown editor.

faq.a: For CommonMark-compatible elements: yes. For HTML-only features (custom CSS, complex layouts), no — Markdown is intentionally a subset.
Not yet. Tables become plain text. We may add GFM table conversion in a future version.

faq.q: Will my links survive the conversion?
faq.a: Yes. text becomes [text](url). The href attribute is preserved.
Stripped. Markdown doesn't support arbitrary styling — that's intentional.

faq.q: How do I convert back to HTML?
faq.a: Use our Markdown to HTML converter (linked under Related Tools).

faq.q: Is the output safe to paste anywhere?
faq.a: It's clean text — no script, no HTML — so safe for any markdown editor or text field. Validate before publishing if input was from an untrusted source.
Custom or non-standard HTML elements may be stripped. Paste an HTML snippet of <details>, <video>, or framework-specific tags to verify.

faq.q: Does the data leave my browser?
faq.a: No. Conversion runs locally; your HTML is never sent anywhere.
